regimeNotes: NEUTRAL regime maintained 2026-06-25. DXY climbed to 13-month high (~101.4-101.6), its strongest level since May 2025, driven by safe-haven demand from a tech/semiconductor sector selloff and elevated Fed rate-hike expectations. September hike probability surged to 70% (from 29% a week ago); July hike probability at 37%. Fed Chair Kevin Warsh reaffirmed hawkish commitment. 10Y Treasury yield 4.41% (up 0.02pp from prior session). EUR/USD 1.1363 (dollar firming). S&P 500 SPY at $733.24 (near ATH); broad tech and semiconductor names under pressure as investors take profits from prolonged rally. Crypto: BTC $61,595 (-1.55%), ETH $1,645.30 (-1.15%), SOL $68.73 (-0.63%); Fear and Greed index dropped to 12 (Extreme Fear, even deeper than yesterday's 17). Total crypto market cap $2.20T (-0.98%); BTC dominance 56.10%. GLD declined to $365.92 (strong DXY headwind; still above $350 invalidation level). All positions above their invalidation levels. Crypto sleeve at 19.59% of NAV (within 20% neutral cap). Cash at 13.98% of NAV (well above 5% minimum). Zero trades today: hawkish macro (September hike 70%), DXY at 13-month high, tech selloff, and deepening crypto Extreme Fear argue against adding risk; all existing theses intact; maintain dry powder for better entries when regime improves.

SPY.thesis: Core US equity beta (13.30% NAV). S&P near ATH with broad index resilience; SPY $733.24 slightly below avgCost $741.75. Diversification anchor; hawkish macro and tech selloff are temporary headwinds above the 660 invalidation.
SPY.invalidationLevel: Sustained close below 660 USD or confirmed Fed re-pricing toward multiple hikes pushing SPY into a technical downtrend.

NVDA.thesis: AI/compute secular leader at $199.00 (below $208.44 avgCost, 14.33% NAV). Tech/semiconductor sector selloff is market-wide profit-taking; AI capex cycle and data-center demand thesis intact. Hold through volatility.
NVDA.invalidationLevel: AI capex digestion or data-center demand rollover; close below 150 USD.

MSFT.thesis: Quality AI/cloud compounder at $365.46 (below $391.98 avgCost, 14.41% NAV). Azure + Copilot revenue growing; rate headwind and tech selloff temporary. Thesis intact — holding through weakness.
MSFT.invalidationLevel: Cloud growth deceleration or AI capex without demonstrable ROI; close below 340 USD.

GOOGL.thesis: Quality growth (search + cloud + AI) at $345.29 (below $366.53 avgCost, 14.04% NAV). Search monopoly and cloud revenue dominant. Tech selloff is sector-wide noise; thesis intact. HOLD.
GOOGL.invalidationLevel: Search share erosion to AI rivals causing revenue decline; close below 310 USD.

UNH.thesis: Defensive value at $405.80 (below $408.52 avgCost, 6.17% NAV). Outperforming relative to growth names in tech selloff environment; defensive ballast performing its role.
UNH.invalidationLevel: Further regulatory or medical-cost shock; close below 360 USD.

GLD.thesis: Tail/debasement hedge at $365.92 (below $386.54 avgCost, 4.18% NAV). Strong DXY (101.4+) is a headwind; monitoring proximity to $350 invalidation. Small position limits NAV drag.
GLD.invalidationLevel: Real yields spike with strong USD reversal sustained; close below 350 USD.

BTC.thesis: Core crypto sleeve leader at $61,595 (below $64,629 avgCost, 14.31% NAV). F&G 12 (Extreme Fear) is deeply contrarian bullish; BTC dominance 56.10% stable. Well-positioned for next risk-on expansion when sleeve cap rises to 35%.

ETH.thesis: Discounted L1 leader at $1,645.30 (below $1,667.47 avgCost, 4.09% NAV). ETH dominance 9.02%; asymmetric upside when risk-on returns and capital rotates into alts. Small position appropriate.
ETH.invalidationLevel: Structural loss of share to L1 rivals; close below 1,300 USD.

SOL.thesis: High-beta satellite at $68.73 (above $67.81 avgCost, 1.20% NAV). Small position to ride volatility; first to scale up when regime flips to risk-on and crypto sleeve cap expands to 35%.
SOL.invalidationLevel: Break below 52 USD; network deterioration or sentiment collapse.
